sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 3, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Black background with two cross/plus shapes: a yellow one (left, ~row 27, col 21) and a blue one (right, ~row 45, col 36) - 8 gray 3x3 squares scattered around: 4 with yellow center dots, 4 with blue center dots - White dot at center of blue cross (~row 45, col 36) - possibly the player cursor - Purple bar at bottom row (64 pixels) - likely HUD/timer - Valid actions: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE
- Goal model: - Move the white dot to collect matching colored dots from gray squares (yellow dots when on yellow cross, blue dots when on blue cross)
- Action model: - SPACE switches cursor between cross centers - UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT likely move the white dot
- Plan: - Try UP to see if the white dot moves upward from its current position at the yellow cross center
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 2 of about 26 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the pattern more clearly. After pressing UP:
- The white dot moved from (27, 21) to (24, 21) - moved UP along the cross
- The horizontal bar of the cross moved from row 27 to row 24
- A gray square with a yellow dot appeared at (17, 15) - this is where the white dot was before? No...
Wait, let me look more carefully at what happened:
- The cross horizontal bar is now at row 24 (was row 27)
- The white dot is at (24, 21) - center of the horizontal bar
- A gray square with yellow dot appeared at (17, 15) - node 9 (c, 8px) with node 11 (Y, 1px)
- The yellow segment that was at (21, 21) - node 7 (Y, 11px) - is now at (18, 21)
So it seems like when I press UP:
1. The entire cross moves UP by 3 rows
2. The white dot stays at the center of the cross
3. As the cross moves, it leaves behind a gray square with a colored dot at its old position
Wait, but the gray square appeared at (17, 15), not at (27, 21) where the cross was. Let me reconsider.
Actually, looking at the ascii:
```
Row 16: cccBBBBYBBBBBBBB
Row 17: cYcBBBBYBBBBBBBB
Row 18: cccBBBBYBBBBBBBB
```
There's a gray square with a yellow dot at rows 16-18, cols 14-16 (center at 17, 15).
And the cross horizontal bar is at row 24.
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe the cross doesn't move as a whole. Maybe pressing UP moves the white dot UP along the vertical arm of the cross, and when it reaches a certain point, the horizontal bar moves to that row.
Actually wait - let me look at the initial state again. The initial yellow cross had:
- Vertical arm at col 21, from row ~18 to ~36
- Horizontal arm at row 27, from col ~14 to ~28
After SPACE (which moved the white dot to the yellow cross):
- The cross structure remained but the white dot was at (27, 21)
After UP:
- White dot at (24, 21)
- Horizontal bar at row 24
- A gray square with yellow dot at (17, 15)
So it seems like UP moved the white dot up 3 rows along the vertical arm, and the horizontal bar followed. But where did the gray square come from?
Let me check: was there a gray square at (17, 15) before? Looking at the initial state:
- Node 8: c, 8px, center (17, 15), children=[11]
- Node 11: Y, 1px, center (17, 15)
Yes! There was a gray square with a yellow dot at (17, 15) from the beginning. After SPACE, this gray square was still there. After UP, it's still there. So the gray square didn't move.
Let me re-examine. After SPACE:
- Node 17: W, 1px, center (27, 21) - white dot at yellow cross center
After UP:
- Node 15: W, 1px, center (24, 21) - white dot moved up
- Node 9: c, 8px, center (17, 15) - this was previously Y, 11px at (21, 21)
So node 9 changed from being a yellow segment (Y, 11px) at (21, 21) to being a gray square (c, 8px) at (17, 15). This is confusing because node 8 was already at (17, 15).
Wait, let me re-read the diff more carefully:
- Node 9: Y->c, 11->8 px, center (21,21) -> (17,15)
- Node 14: b->Y, 1->11 px, center (24,40) -> (24,15)
- Node 15: b->W, 1->1 px, center (24,53) -> (24,21)
- Node 17: W->b, 1->1 px, center (27,21) -> (24,40)
- Node 18: Y->b, 11->1 px, center (27,27) -> (24,53)
So nodes are swapping positions and colors! It's like the game is rotating/swapping the positions of certain elements.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the game involves:
1. Moving the white dot along the cross
2. When the white dot reaches a gray square, it "collects" the dot and the square disappears or changes
Let me try pressing UP again to see if the pattern continues, and also try to understand the relationship between the white dot position and the gray squares.
Actually, I notice that the white dot moved from (27, 21) to (24, 21), which is exactly 3 rows up. The gray square at (17, 15) is 7 rows above and 6 columns left of the new white dot position.
Let me just try a few more movements to understand the pattern better. Let me try UP again.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- Two crosses: yellow (left) and blue (right/bottom)
- White dot is the player cursor, currently at (24, 21) on the yellow cross
- 8 gray 3x3 squares with colored center dots scattered around
- SPACE moved white dot from blue cross to yellow cross center
- UP moved white dot up 3 rows along the cross arm, horizontal bar followed
- Purple bar at bottom shrinks each action (HUD)
- Movement causes complex node swaps/transformations
Goal model:
- Move white dot to gray squares to collect colored dots
Action model:
- SPACE: toggle cursor between cross centers
- UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT: move white dot along cross arms
Plan:
- Try UP again to continue moving up and see if I can reach a gray square
